title: PagedAttention & vLLM
learning_outcomes: Explain PagedAttention virtual memory block maps.
Formulate KV Cache block allocation equations.

content_sections.title: KV Cache Fragmentation and PagedAttention
content_sections.content:
Traditional KV Cache allocation requires continuous memory blocks allocated for the maximum sequence length. This leads to severe memory fragmentation (wasting up to 60-80% of RAM). 

**PagedAttention** solves this by partitioning the KV Cache into fixed-size physical blocks, mapping them to virtual blocks using a page table. This eliminates fragmentation and allows KV caches to be shared across requests.
